name: llm_synthesized_cJSON_to_json-rust_public_v1

cjson_add_bool_should_add_bool.description: cJSON add bool to object and retrieve via GetObjectItemCaseSensitive, verify type
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 446: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(true_item = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "true")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(true_item->type, cJSON_True); similarly for false.

cjson_add_false_should_add_false.description: cJSON add false to object and retrieve via GetObjectItemCaseSensitive, verify type
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 442: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(false_item = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "false")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(false_item->type, cJSON_False);

cjson_add_array_should_add_array.description: Create object, add empty array via insert, retrieve and verify type is array
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 466: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(array = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(...)), TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(array->type, cJSON_Array)

cjson_add_null_should_add_null.description: Create object, add null value via insert, retrieve and verify null type
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 429: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(null = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(...)), TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(null->type, cJSON_NULL)

cjson_add_null_should_fail_on_allocation.description: cJSON_AddNullToObject returns NULL on allocation failure when failing_hooks set
cjson_add_null_should_fail_on_allocation.normalization: null pointer comparison (source) vs error check (target)
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 431: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_AddNullToObject(root, "null"))

cjson_add_number_should_add_number.description: cJSON_AddNumberToObject adds a number with value 42
cjson_add_number_should_add_number.normalization: value comparison via as_f64 and as_i32
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 450-451: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(number = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "number")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE(number->valuedouble, 42); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(number->valueint, 42);

cjson_add_number_should_fail_on_allocati.description: cJSON_AddNumberToObject returns NULL on allocation failure when failing_hooks set
cjson_add_number_should_fail_on_allocati.normalization: null pointer comparison (source) vs error check (target)
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 452: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "number", 42));

cjson_add_object_should_add_object.normalization: type check via pattern matching on JsonValue::Object
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 462-463: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(object = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "object")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(object->type, cJSON_Object);

cjson_add_object_should_fail_on_allocati.description: cJSON_AddObjectToObject returns NULL on allocation failure when failing_hooks set
cjson_add_object_should_fail_on_allocati.normalization: null pointer comparison (source) vs error check (target)
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 464: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_AddObjectToObject(root, "object"));

cjson_add_raw_should_add_raw.description: cJSON_AddRawToObject adds a raw JSON string
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 458-459: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(raw = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "raw")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(raw->type, cJSON_Raw); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING(raw->valuestring, "{}");

cjson_add_raw_should_fail_on_allocation_.description: cJSON_AddRawToObject returns NULL on allocation failure when failing_hooks set
cjson_add_raw_should_fail_on_allocation_.normalization: null pointer comparison (source) vs error check (target)
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 460: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_AddRawToObject(root, "raw", "{}"));

cjson_add_string_should_add_string.description: add a string to an object and verify retrieval
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 454-460: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING

cjson_add_true_should_add_true.description: add a boolean true to an object and verify retrieval
evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 433-438: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT
tests/compare_tests.c lines 204-: multiple TEST_ASSERT_TRUE from compare_from_string helper
tests/compare_tests.c lines 199-: multiple TEST_ASSERT_TRUE

cjson_compare_should_compare_invalid_as_.description: Compare two invalid cJSON items should be not equal
evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 197: TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(cJSON_Compare(invalid, invalid, false)) and TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(cJSON_Compare(invalid, invalid, true))
tests/compare_tests.c line 200: compare_from_string("null", "null", true) should be true

cjson_compare_should_compare_null_pointe.description: Compare two null pointers should be not equal
evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 196: TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(cJSON_Compare(NULL, NULL, true)) and TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(cJSON_Compare(NULL, NULL, false))
tests/compare_tests.c line 198: compare_from_string("1", "1", true) true and compare_from_string("0.0001", "0.0001", true) true

cjson_compare_should_compare_objects.description: Compare objects with same and different keys
evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 205: compare_from_string("{}", "{}", true) true
tests/compare_tests.c line 203: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(cJSON_Compare(raw1, raw2, true)) and TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(cJSON_Compare(raw1, raw2, false))

cjson_compare_should_compare_strings.description: Compare strings with and without case sensitivity
evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 202: compare_from_string("\"abcdefg\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true) true

cjson_compare_should_not_accept_invalid_.description: Compare items with invalid type should return false
evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 201: TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(cJSON_Compare(invalid, invalid, true)) and TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(cJSON_Compare(invalid, invalid, false))

cjson_add_item_to_object_should_not_use_.description: Add item to object with string aliased to item's string member
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 831: cJSON_AddItemToObject(object, number->string, number) no use-after-free

cjson_add_item_to_object_or_array_should.description: add object or array to itself should fail
cjson_add_item_to_object_or_array_should.normalization: return value false maps to Result::Err
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c lines 6-10: TEST_ASSERT_FALSE_MESSAGE on adding object to itself and array to itself

cjson_delete_item_from_array_should_not_.description: Delete an item from an array inside an object and verify serialization matches expected strings
cjson_delete_item_from_array_should_not_.normalization: exact string equality of JSON output
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 832: TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING(expected_json1, str1); etc.

cjson_get_number_value_should_get_a_numb.description: Test that as_f64 returns Some(f64) for a number and None for a string
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 826: TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE(cJSON_GetNumberValue(number), number->valuedouble); TEST_ASSERT_DOUBLE_IS_NAN(cJSON_GetNumberValue(string));

cjson_get_object_item_case_sensitive_sho.description: Test case-sensitive object item lookup for existing and missing keys
cjson_get_object_item_case_sensitive_sho.normalization: Resulting JsonValue is checked via is_null() and as_f64()
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 809: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL_MESSAGE and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE for 'one'; TEST_ASSERT_NULL_MESSAGE for 'One'

cjson_get_object_item_case_sensitive_sho.description: Test that index on an array does not panic and returns Null
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 811: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(found);

cjson_get_object_item_should_get_object_.description: Test case-insensitive object item lookup (target only provides case-sensitive, exposing difference)
cjson_get_object_item_should_get_object_.normalization: Result is null (target returns Null for case-mismatched keys)
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 808: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL_MESSAGE for 'tWo'; source expects non-null for case-insensitive match

cjson_get_object_item_should_not_crash_w.description: Test that index on array returns Null without crash
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 810: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(found);

cjson_get_string_value_should_get_a_stri.description: Test that as_str returns Some(&str) for a string and None for a number
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 825: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(cJSON_GetStringValue(string) == string->valuestring); TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_GetStringValue(number));

misc_tests_cjson_parse_big_numbers_shoul.description: Parse valid and invalid big number JSON strings
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 835: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL on valid big number parse, TEST_ASSERT_NULL_MESSAGE on invalid

misc_tests_cjson_replace_item_in_object_.description: Replace item in object preserves key name
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 819: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(root->child == replacement) and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING("child", replacement->string)

misc_tests_cjson_should_not_parse_to_dee.description: Parse deeply nested JSON should fail
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 813: TEST_ASSERT_NULL_MESSAGE(cJSON_Parse(deep_json), ...)

misc_tests_typecheck_functions_should_ch.description: Type-checking functions return correct results for various JSON types
evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 812: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE/FALSE on typecheck functions

cjson_parse_should_not_parse_incomplete_.description: Should fail to parse incomplete JSON string
evidence: source test asserts tree is NULL and error pointer is at end of string; Rust replay asserts parse returns Err

cjson_parse_with_opts_should_parse_utf8_.description: Should parse JSON with UTF-8 BOM and compare equal to without BOM
evidence: source test parses BOM and non-BOM JSON strings and asserts cJSON_Compare returns true

parse_examples_file_test4_should_be_pars.description: Parse a JSON file (test4) and print the tree, verifying non-null results as derived from source assertions
evidence: tests/parse_examples.c: file_test4_should_be_parsed_and_printed asserts tree != NULL, actual != NULL, actual matches expected (replayed as not_null on parse result and non-empty pretty output)

_behavior_case_coverage.event_source_case_id_policy: Only explicit trace_events[].source_case_ids count as behavior-case replay coverage. Function-name fallback is intentionally not used because one function can have many source test behavior variants.
